name: Brunswick Stew I
recipeIngredient: 6 lb Stewing hen (or two 3 lb broilers)
4 cups Fresh Tomatoes (or 2 cans of tomatoes, 1 lb each)
4 cups Corn from cob (or 2 cans of corn, 1 lb each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into pieces and simmer in 3 quarts of water for a thin stew or 2 quarts of water for a thick stew until the meat can easily be removed from the bones, about 2 1/4 hours.
2. Remove the chicken and let it cool.
3. Add the vegetables to the broth and simmer uncovered until the beans and potatoes are tender. Stir occasionally to prevent scorching.
4. Add the deboned and diced chicken or in sections.
5. Add the seasonings.
Note: If canned vegetables are used, include their juices and reduce water to 2 quarts for a thin stew and 1 quart for a thick stew.
Also note: Brunswick Stew is one of those delectable things that benefit from long, slow cooking. It is a rule in some homes never to eat Brunswick Stew the same day it is made because its flavor improves if it is left to stand overnight and is reheated the next day.
